body:
## Summary
- Add `Agent / Onboarding / Check Setup` for first-run setup checks.
- Create built-in `agent/*` trigger labels and maintain one `Sepo setup check` issue/comment.
- Document the onboarding flow in the README and setup docs.

## Verification
- `npm --prefix .agent test`
- `ruby -e 'require "yaml"; Dir[".github/workflows/*.yml"].sort.each { |file| YAML.load_file(file) }'`

Closes #23

comments.body:
Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `implement` -> `review` (round 3/10).

agent planner selected review: Implementation succeeded and produced PR #62 for issue #23; an automated review is the appropriate next step before merge.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777531983099 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUxNTE1MzEwMzU6aW1wbGVtZW50OjIzOnJldmlldzo2Mjoz -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 7 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR closes #23, whose discussion requested a first-run onboarding issue, setup status, next steps, and copyable commands; the diff implements that shape in `.github/workflows/agent-onboarding.yml` and `.agent/src/onboarding.ts`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The CLI reuses existing `.agent/src/github.ts` helpers: `createIssue`, `ensureLabel`, `gh`, and `postIssueComment`, and the workflow reuses shared `resolve-github-auth`, `resolve-agent-provider`, and `setup-agent-runtime` actions. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | README, quick start, install docs, and supported workflows docs were updated to describe `Agent / Onboarding / Check Setup`, trigger label creation, and the setup issue. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | The new workflow exposes separate typed `memory_ref` and `rubrics_ref` inputs instead of overloading one string, and the CLI consumes explicit env fields for auth/provider/ref status. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The diff is scoped to onboarding: one workflow, onboarding CLI/module, trigger-label constants, tests, and directly related documentation. No broad refactors or unrelated formatting churn are visible. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The implementation is straightforward: ensure labels, check branch refs, find/create one setup issue, then post or patch one marker comment. No extra orchestration or unnecessary abstraction was added. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | Documentation additions describe the onboarding workflow behavior and update existing workflow/setup sections; they do not introduce per-file inventory tables. |

## Notes

- The strongest rubric alignment is reuse and docs sync: PR #62 follows the plan in issue #23 by keeping workflow glue thin and putting the GitHub mutation logic in typed `.agent/src` modules with focused CLI tests.
- Authorization and orchestrator follow-up rubrics were not scored because this PR does not change route delegation, downstream authorization checks, or follow-up orchestration behavior. It only creates labels that existing label routing will later process.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific findings. The PR satisfies the applicable selected rubrics.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #62 adds a first-run `Agent / Onboarding / Check Setup` workflow for Sepo installs. It creates built-in `agent/*` trigger labels, checks auth/provider/memory/rubrics readiness, and opens or updates a single `Sepo setup check` issue with copyable test commands. This addresses issue #23’s request to make initial setup more visible and practical after installation. The implementation keeps workflow glue thin and puts most GitHub mutation logic in typed `.agent/src` code with tests and docs updates.

## Review
Overall, the PR is close and matches the requested onboarding direction. One workflow default issue can make repository-configured branch refs unreachable, so it should be fixed before merge.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Workflow defaults make configured branch refs unreachable | WARNING | `workflow_dispatch` input defaults make `inputs.memory_ref` and `inputs.rubrics_ref` non-empty, so the `vars.AGENT_MEMORY_REF` / `vars.AGENT_RUBRICS_REF` fallbacks are effectively dead. |
| Built-in trigger labels have two sources of truth | INFO | `BUILT_IN_TRIGGER_LABELS` duplicates route knowledge from `resolveRequestedLabel()`, which could drift when routes change. |

## Progress
- Rubrics review passed and found the PR aligned with reuse, docs sync, surgical changes, and structured-input expectations.
- The implementation already includes docs updates and focused CLI tests.
- One inline comment was posted on `.github/workflows/agent-onboarding.yml` line 28 for the workflow default issue.
- No existing inline comments duplicated this finding.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Workflow defaults make configured branch refs unreachable</summary>

**Cause:** The workflow defines defaults for `memory_ref` and `rubrics_ref`, then later tries to fall back to repository vars with `${{ inputs.memory_ref || vars.AGENT_MEMORY_REF || 'agent/memory' }}`. On normal manual dispatches, the input defaults are already populated, so repository vars are skipped.

**Candidate solutions:** Remove the input defaults so repository vars can apply, or remove the vars fallback and document that these refs are controlled only by explicit dispatch inputs.

**Comments:** This affects repositories that customize memory or rubrics branch names through vars; the setup issue could report the wrong branch status.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Built-in trigger labels have two sources of truth</summary>

**Cause:** `.agent/src/trigger-labels.ts` defines built-in labels separately from the router logic in `triage.ts`.

**Candidate solutions:** Share one built-in route/label map with the resolver, or add a focused test proving every `BUILT_IN_TRIGGER_LABELS` entry resolves through `resolveRequestedLabel()`.

**Comments:** This is not blocking for the current PR because the list appears aligned now, but it is a drift risk.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: the workflow default issue is concrete, small, and safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Fix `.github/workflows/agent-onboarding.yml` so repository vars for `AGENT_MEMORY_REF` and `AGENT_RUBRICS_REF` are reachable, or remove/document the vars fallback behavior.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 4/10).

agent planner selected fix-pr: Review synthesis recommends FIX_PR and the remaining blocking item is a concrete, small workflow bug: dispatch input defaults make AGENT_MEMORY_REF and AGENT_RUBRICS_REF vars unreachable.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777532448272 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUxNTE3NDI0MzI6cmV2aWV3OjYyOmZpeF9wcjo2Mjo0 -->

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/implement-issue-23/codex-25151531035`. Requested by @sepo-agent.

- Removed `workflow_dispatch` defaults for `memory_ref` and `rubrics_ref` in `.github/workflows/agent-onboarding.yml` so repo vars `AGENT_MEMORY_REF` and `AGENT_RUBRICS_REF` can take effect.
- Preserved the existing expression fallbacks to `agent/memory` and `agent/rubrics`.
- Verified with YAML parsing and `npm --prefix .agent test` (415 tests passed).

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `fix-pr` -> `review` (round 5/10).

agent planner selected review: fix-pr succeeded after the prior review recommended FIX_PR; PR #62 now has a new commit removing onboarding workflow dispatch defaults so repository ref vars should be reachable. A fresh automated review is warranted to verify the fix and reassess remaining issues before stopping.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777532593145 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUxNTIwMjE2MzI6Zml4X3ByOjYyOnJldmlldzo2Mjo1 -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 7 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR closes #23, which requested first-run setup checks, missing configuration status, next steps, and copyable commands; the diff implements that onboarding flow. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The workflow reuses existing local actions for auth/provider/runtime setup, and `.agent/src/onboarding.ts` reuses `ensureLabel`, `createIssue`, `gh`, and `postIssueComment` from `.agent/src/github.ts`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | README, quick start, install docs, and supported workflow docs were updated for `Agent / Onboarding / Check Setup`, trigger labels, and the setup issue behavior. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | The workflow exposes separate `memory_ref` and `rubrics_ref` inputs, with separate `MEMORY_REF` and `RUBRICS_REF` env fields and clear fallbacks to repo vars/default branches. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Changes are scoped to onboarding setup: one workflow, one CLI/module, trigger-label metadata, focused tests, and directly related docs. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The implementation is direct and idempotent: ensure labels, check branches, find/create one setup issue, then post or patch one marker comment. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | Documentation additions describe setup behavior and update existing workflow docs without adding a new per-file inventory table. |

## Notes

- The earlier workflow default issue has been fixed in the current PR diff: `memory_ref` and `rubrics_ref` no longer define dispatch defaults, so `vars.AGENT_MEMORY_REF` and `vars.AGENT_RUBRICS_REF` remain reachable.
- Authorization delegation, follow-up orchestration defaults, preview contracts, merge strategy, and workflow-stop comment rubrics were not scored because this PR does not change those behaviors.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific findings. The current PR satisfies the applicable selected rubrics.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #62 adds a first-run `Agent / Onboarding / Check Setup` workflow for Sepo installs.
- It creates built-in `agent/*` trigger labels, checks auth/provider/memory/rubrics readiness, and opens or updates a single `Sepo setup check` issue with copyable commands.
- The PR addresses issue #23’s onboarding request: make setup state visible, create missing trigger labels, and give new users clear next steps.
- The current head includes the follow-up fix that removed `workflow_dispatch` defaults so repository ref vars remain reachable.

## Review
The current PR is in good shape. The previously identified workflow-default issue is fixed, tests and docs are in place, and the only remaining note is a non-blocking drift risk around duplicated route-label metadata.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Built-in trigger labels have a second route map | INFO | `BUILT_IN_TRIGGER_LABELS` duplicates built-in routing knowledge from `resolveRequestedLabel()`. The lists are aligned today, but a future route change could update one side without the other. |

## Progress
- The earlier `memory_ref` / `rubrics_ref` default issue has been fixed in the current PR head.
- Rubrics review passed with no remaining rubric-specific findings.
- Existing discussion already covers the prior workflow-default finding and the non-blocking trigger-label drift note, so no new inline comments were posted.
- Reviewer verification reported `npm --prefix .agent test` passing with 415 tests after installing `.agent` dependencies.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Built-in trigger labels have a second route map</summary>

**Cause:** `.agent/src/trigger-labels.ts` defines built-in labels separately from the router logic in `.agent/src/triage.ts` via `resolveRequestedLabel()`.

**Candidate solutions:** Derive both onboarding labels and route resolution from a shared built-in label map, or add a focused regression test proving each `BUILT_IN_TRIGGER_LABELS` entry resolves through `resolveRequestedLabel()`.

**Comments:** This is not blocking because the current labels appear aligned and this is a future drift risk, not a current behavior bug.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION: there is no required branch-change work remaining; the only note is optional maintainability hardening.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change action items.

@sepo-agent /answer 

1. What are the updates that will be posted on the first issue? 
2. Second, that doesn't need any API access right? 
3. Further, is there specific instruction for users to conduct certain tasks or asks after the setup?

Based on PR #62:

1. The first issue will be titled `Sepo setup check`. It includes copyable test prompts for:
   - `@sepo-agent /answer Is Sepo configured correctly in this repository?`
   - `@sepo-agent /implement Create a small README update that verifies the agent can open a PR.`
   - `@sepo-agent /review`

   The workflow then posts or updates one checklist comment with:
   - GitHub auth status
   - agent provider status
   - whether `OPENAI_API_KEY` is configured
   - whether `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` is configured
   - whether `agent/memory` exists
   - whether `agent/rubrics` exists
   - confirmation that built-in trigger labels were ensured
   - the built-in label list: `agent/answer`, `agent/implement`, `agent/create-action`, `agent/review`, `agent/fix-pr`, `agent/orchestrate`
   - next steps for missing auth/provider/memory/rubrics setup

   See the issue body and checklist generation in [`onboarding.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/agent/implement-issue-23/codex-25151531035/.agent/src/onboarding.ts#L102-L156).

2. It does **not** need model API access to run the onboarding check. The workflow only checks whether provider credentials are configured; it does not run Codex or Claude. It does need GitHub access because it creates labels, creates/updates the setup issue, checks branch refs, and posts/patches comments. The workflow permissions are `contents: read`, `issues: write`, and `id-token: write`; it uses resolved GitHub auth or the workflow token fallback. See [`agent-onboarding.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/agent/implement-issue-23/codex-25151531035/.github/workflows/agent-onboarding.yml#L13-L69).

3. Yes. The user-facing flow after setup is:
   - Run `Agent / Onboarding / Check Setup`.
   - Review the `Sepo setup check` issue.
   - Run one of the copyable commands from that issue, or open another issue and mention `@sepo-agent`.
   - Wait for the eyes reaction and follow-up workflow run.

   The setup issue also tells users to run `Agent / Memory / Initialization` if the memory branch is missing, and `Agent / Rubrics / Initialization` if the rubrics branch is missing and they want team rubrics. This is documented in [`install-existing-repository.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/agent/implement-issue-23/codex-25151531035/.agent/docs/deployment/install-existing-repository.md#L38-L50).

Is "Run `Agent / Onboarding / Check Setup" executed automatically or that needs manual execution?  

@sepo-agent /answer

It needs **manual execution**.

In PR #62, `Agent / Onboarding / Check Setup` is defined as a `workflow_dispatch` workflow, which means it runs only when someone starts it from GitHub Actions, or when another workflow explicitly dispatches it. It is **not** automatically run on install, push, issue creation, or first mention.

Expected flow after installing Sepo:

1. Go to **Actions**.
2. Select **Agent / Onboarding / Check Setup**.
3. Click **Run workflow**.
4. Review the created/updated `Sepo setup check` issue.
5. Use one of the copyable test prompts from that issue.

It is safe to rerun; reruns update the same setup issue/comment rather than creating duplicates.

> It needs **manual execution**.
> 
> In PR #62, `Agent / Onboarding / Check Setup` is defined as a `workflow_dispatch` workflow, which means it runs only when someone starts it from GitHub Actions, or when another workflow explicitly dispatches it. It is **not** automatically run on install, push, issue creation, or first mention.
> 
> Expected flow after installing Sepo:
> 
> 1. Go to **Actions**.
> 2. Select **Agent / Onboarding / Check Setup**.
> 3. Click **Run workflow**.
> 4. Review the created/updated `Sepo setup check` issue.
> 5. Use one of the copyable test prompts from that issue.
> 
> It is safe to rerun; reruns update the same setup issue/comment rather than creating duplicates.

@sepo-agent /answer 
But can we actually automatically run this when one just fork a repo or set up ours in their own repo?

I’ll answer this at the GitHub Actions behavior level: whether there is an event Sepo can rely on, and what would be needed if we want a more automatic first-run path.Yes, but not automatically in the current PR, and not reliably from **just forking** with GitHub Actions alone.

The practical split is:

1. **When someone forks the repo**
   - GitHub does not give the new fork a dependable “first run in this fork” workflow trigger that can immediately create issues/labels inside the fork.
   - A `fork` event exists on the **source/upstream** repo, not as a setup event running inside the new fork.
   - Also, Actions may require the fork owner to enable workflows, and secrets/app credentials will not be configured yet.
   - So for forks, the safest default is still: user enables Actions/configures secrets, then manually runs `Agent / Onboarding / Check Setup`.

2. **When someone installs/copies Sepo into their own repo**
   - We *could* make this more automatic by adding a `push` trigger to `agent-onboarding.yml`, probably limited to the default branch and guarded so it only creates/updates the setup issue once.
   - Example behavior: after `.agent/` and `.github/` are merged into the target repo, the next push to `main` runs onboarding automatically.
   - This would still require GitHub issue/label write permission via `GITHUB_TOKEN`, GitHub App auth, or PAT, because creating labels/issues/comments uses the GitHub API.

3. **If we want true automatic setup on install/fork**
   - That would need an external GitHub App/webhook flow: on app installation or repo creation, the app creates the setup issue or dispatches the onboarding workflow.
   - That is outside plain repo-local GitHub Actions and would require API access from the app/backend.

My recommendation: keep the current manual `workflow_dispatch` as the reliable baseline, and optionally add a guarded `push` trigger for newly installed repos. I would not rely on fork-time automation unless we add a GitHub App installation webhook path.

Yes. The clean version is: the **Sepo GitHub App backend** listens for GitHub App installation events, then bootstraps the installed repository by either creating the setup issue directly or dispatching `Agent / Onboarding / Check Setup`.

Existing pieces already line up with this:

- Sepo already treats GitHub App auth as a first-class path via `.github/actions/resolve-github-auth/action.yml`, which resolves direct app credentials, hosted OIDC broker, PAT, or `github.token`.
- Workflow dispatch already exists as a repo helper in `.agent/src/github.ts` through `dispatchWorkflow()`.
- Existing orchestration already uses workflow dispatch plus dedupe markers, documented in `.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md`.
- The app permission model already says `Actions: read/write` is needed for workflow dispatch and `Issues: read/write` for issue/comment work in `.agent/docs/deployment/using-your-own-github-app.md`.

The implementation shape I’d propose:

1. Add a small hosted webhook endpoint for the Sepo GitHub App.
   - Receive GitHub `installation` and `installation_repositories` events.
   - Verify `X-Hub-Signature-256` with the app webhook secret.
   - Extract `installation.id` and the selected repositories.

2. For each installed repo, mint a GitHub App installation token.
   - Use the Sepo App private key server-side.
   - Token needs at least `metadata: read`, `issues: write`, and optionally `actions: write`.

3. Check whether the repo actually contains Sepo.
   - Look for `.github/workflows/agent-onboarding.yml` on the default branch.
   - If missing, create a lightweight issue saying “Sepo App is installed, but the repo files are not installed yet.”
   - If present, continue.

4. Prefer dispatching the onboarding workflow.
   - Call GitHub’s workflow dispatch endpoint for `agent-onboarding.yml`.
   - Use the repo default branch as `ref`.
   - This lets the repo-local workflow perform the real checks: auth mode, provider secrets, memory/rubrics refs, labels, and the setup issue.

5. Fall back to direct issue creation if dispatch fails.
   - Common failures: Actions disabled, workflow file missing on default branch, app lacks `actions: write`.
   - The app can still create/update `Sepo setup check` directly with instructions to enable Actions and run the workflow manually.

6. Add idempotency.
   - Search for an open `Sepo setup check` issue or a hidden marker comment before creating anything.
   - Store a marker like `<!-- sepo-agent-installation-bootstrap -->`.
   - Reinstallation or adding more repositories should update the same issue/comment, not spam duplicates.

7. Document the behavior.
   - “Installing the Sepo App will try to start onboarding automatically.”
   - “If Actions are disabled or Sepo files are missing, the app posts a setup issue instead.”
   - “Provider secrets still must be configured by the repository owner.”

Important limitation: the App installation webhook cannot read or configure repository Actions secrets like `OPENAI_API_KEY`. It can only detect installation, create labels/issues, dispatch workflows, and guide the user. The repo-local onboarding workflow is still the right place to check provider readiness because it runs inside the target repo’s Actions context.

I’d implement this as a hosted bootstrap path with **dispatch-first, issue-fallback** behavior. That gives the smoothest onboarding without making fork/template setup depend on a fragile GitHub Actions event.

title: Add first-run onboarding setup workflow
